query: Can you suggest a corpus that contains French encyclopedia documents with semantic annotations and includes a test set of manually written question/answer triplets that align with the constraints of FrameNet semantic analysis?
criteria: French encyclopedia corpus with FrameNet semantic annotations
French FrameNet-annotated corpus including question-answer triplets
Manually written QA test set for French FrameNet semantic analysis

title.value: Developing a French FrameNet: Methodology and First results

recommendation_reason.value: The Asfalda project aims to develop a French corpus with frame-based semantic annotations and automatic tools for shallow semantic analysis.

supported_research_question.value: The Asfalda project aims to develop a French corpus with frame-based semantic annotations and automatic tools for shallow semantic analysis.

method.value: focusing on a set of notional domains, we delimited a subset of English frames, adapted them to French data when necessary, and developed the corresponding French lexicon.

data_or_scenario.value: The Asfalda project aims to develop a French corpus with frame-based semantic annotations and automatic tools for shallow semantic analysis.

main_conclusion.value: We believe that working domain by domain helped us to enforce the coherence of the resulting resource, and also has the advantage that, though the number of frames is limited (around a hundred), we obtain full coverage within a given domain.

limitations.value: The Asfalda project aims to develop a French corpus with frame-based semantic annotations and automatic tools for shallow semantic analysis.

relevance_strength.value: The Asfalda project aims to develop a French corpus with frame-based semantic annotations and automatic tools for shallow semantic analysis.

title.value: A general framework for the annotation of causality based on FrameNet

recommendation_reason.value: We present here a general set of semantic frames to annotate causal expressions, with a rich lexicon in French and an annotated corpus of about 4000 instances of causal lexical items with their corresponding semantic frames.

supported_research_question.value: We present here a general set of semantic frames to annotate causal expressions, with a rich lexicon in French and an annotated corpus of about 4000 instances of causal lexical items with their corresponding semantic frames.

method.value: The aim of our project is to have both the largest possible coverage of causal phenomena in French, across all parts of speech, and have it linked to a general semantic framework such as FN, to benefit in particular from the relations between other semantic frames, e.

data_or_scenario.value: We present here a general set of semantic frames to annotate causal expressions, with a rich lexicon in French and an annotated corpus of about 4000 instances of causal lexical items with their corresponding semantic frames.

main_conclusion.value: The result is arguably a significant improvement of the treatment of causality in FN itself.

limitations.value: We present here a general set of semantic frames to annotate causal expressions, with a rich lexicon in French and an annotated corpus of about 4000 instances of causal lexical items with their corresponding semantic frames.

relevance_strength.value: We present here a general set of semantic frames to annotate causal expressions, with a rich lexicon in French and an annotated corpus of about 4000 instances of causal lexical items with their corresponding semantic frames.

title.value: Corpus annotation within the French FrameNet: a domain-by-domain methodology

recommendation_reason.value: This paper reports on the development of a French FrameNet, within the ASFALDA project.

supported_research_question.value: This paper reports on the development of a French FrameNet, within the ASFALDA project.

method.value: the subsequent corpus annotation phase, which focused on four notional domains (commercial transactions, cognitive stances, causality and verbal communication).

data_or_scenario.value: frames and roles were annotated on two French Treebanks (the French Treebank (Abeillé andBarrier, 2004)and the Sequoia Treebank (Candito and Seddah, 2012)

main_conclusion.value: In the resource's current status, there are 98 frames, 662 frame-evoking words, 872 senses, and about 13000 annotated frames, with their semantic roles assigned to portions of text.

limitations.value: Given full coverage is not reachable for a relatively "new" FrameNet project

relevance_strength.value: This paper reports on the development of a French FrameNet, within the ASFALDA project.
